LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LUELLA GARRETT AND ANTHONY GARRETT'S MOTION TO COMPEL DOCUMENT PRODUCTION AND THIRD PARTY TESTIMONY IS GRANTED IN LARGE PART. THE GARRETTS MAY TAKE THE DEPOSITION OF OFFICER MANASKER AT A MUTUALLY AGREEABLE DATE AND TIME NO LATER THAN AUGUST 22, 2017. OFFICER MANASKER IS REQUIRED TO BRING TO THE DEPOSITION ALL OF THE DOCUMENTS IN THE POSSESSION, CUSTODY AND CONTROL OF THE PACIFICA POLICE DEPARTMENT OR IN HIS POSSESSION, CUSTODY OR CONTROL THAT RELATE TO THE MAY 27, 2014 ACCIDENT AND/OR AN INVESTIGATION WHETHER PLAINTIFF DANA NISHIMURA WAS DRIVING UNDER THE INFLUENCE OF CONTROLLED SUBSTANCES AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT, INCLUDING: A) AN UNREDACTED POLICE REPORT AND B) DOCUMENTATION OF BLOOD SAMPLES TAKEN OF MR. NISHIMURA AFTER THE ACCIDENT. THE GARRETTS HAVE SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR THIS DISCOVERY AND MR. NISHIMURA'S OBJECTIONS BASED ON CCP 1985.3(G) AND HIS PRIVACY RIGHTS LACK MERIT. THE TWENTY DAY TIME PERIOD TO FILE A MOTION TO COMPEL IN SUBSECTION 1985.3(G) ONLY APPLIES WHERE A NON-PARTY HAS SERVED OBJECTIONS TO THE SUBPOENA. PER THE FIRST PARAGRAPH OF THAT SUBSECTION, WHERE, AS HERE, A PARTY OBJECTS TO THE SUBPOENA, THE PARTY IS REQUIRED TO FILE A MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY THE SUBPOENA IF THE PARTY OBJECTS TO THE SUBPOENA. THERE IS NO INDICATION IN THE RECORD ON THIS MOTION THAT MR. NISHIMURA FILED A MOTION TO QUASH OR MODIFY ANY OF THE SUBPOENAS SERVED ON OFFICER MANASKER. WHILE THE DOCUMENTS SOUGHT REGARDING MR. NISHIMURA'S ASSERTED POSSESSION AND USE OF CONTROLLED SUBSTANCES IMPLICATE HIS PRIVACY RIGHTS, THOSE RIGHTS DO NOT PRECLUDE THE DOCUMENTS SOUGHT SINCE THEY SEEK INFORMATION THAT IS DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO THE CLAIMS AND DEFENSES IN THIS CASE. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GORDON F. AIAVAO, CSR NO. 11216, EMAIL: GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Document Production And Third Party Testimony
Off Calendar Aug-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Steven C. Kirby of the law offices of Kirbys Law (310-372--8429) appears as counsel for plaintiff/non-opposing party

Attorney, Robert B. Burchfiel of the law offices of Rankin Stock Haeberlin (408-293-0463) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

Having met and conferred, counsel submit on the tentative ruling without argument.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS LUELLA GARRETT and ANTHONY GARRETT's Motion To Compel Document Production And Third Party Testimony is GRANTED in large part. The Garretts may take the deposition of Officer Manasker at a mutually agreeable date and time no later than August 22, 2017. Officer Manasker is required to bring to the deposition all of the documents in the possession, custody and control of the Pacifica Police Department or in his possession, custody or control that relate to the May 27, 2014 accident and/or an investigation whether plaintiff Dana Nishimura was driving under the influence of controlled substances at the time of the accident, including: a) an unredacted police report and b) documentation of blood samples taken of Mr. Nishimura after the accident. The Garretts have shown good cause for this discovery and Mr. Nishimura's objections based on CCP 1985.3(g) and his privacy rights lack merit. The twenty day time period to file a motion to compel in subsection 1985.3(g) only applies where a non-party has served objections to the subpoena. Per the first paragraph of that subsection, where, as here, a party objects to the subpoena, the party is required to file a motion to quash or modify the subpoena if the party objects to the subpoena. There is no indication in the record on this motion that Mr. Nishimura filed a motion to quash or modify any of the subpoenas served on Officer Manasker. While the documents sought regarding Mr. Nishimura's asserted possession and use of controlled substances implicate his privacy rights, those rights do not preclude the documents sought since they seek information that is directly relevant to the claims and defenses in this case. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)
